skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide for creating and refining AI skills with structured instructions, reusable resources, templates, validation, and packaging workflows.
skill.summary: Create, validate, and package AI skills using templates and practical workflow guidance.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk indicators, but review found the weak-crypto and shell-execution alerts are false positives from license prose, Markdown templates, and code examples. The real risk is limited to local filesystem scripts that create, validate, and package skill directories; no malicious intent, credential access, or data exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Operations in Helper Scripts
low_findings.description: The helper scripts create skill directories, write template files, read SKILL.md, and package files into a zip archive. This is expected for a skill creation tool, but users should run it only on intended local paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed code directly performs local file and archive operations. The operations are consistent with the stated skill purpose and do not show exfiltration or destructive behavior.

low_findings.title: License URLs Are Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are Apache License reference links in LICENSE.txt. They are not fetched by any script and do not create runtime network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs appear inside standard license text. No code path uses them for network requests.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto Alerts Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts occur in prose, YAML, placeholders, or Markdown examples, not in cryptographic code. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, or insecure hashing use was found in reviewed executable logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations are documentation or validation text rather than cryptographic implementations. The scanner appears to match short tokens in Markdown and prose.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code, fenced examples, or template placeholders. The Python scripts do not call subprocess, shell, eval, or backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting or string template content. Review found no executable shell invocation in the relevant Python scripts.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Output Path Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: init-skill.py writes generated files under a user-supplied path. This is legitimate behavior for an initializer, but misuse could create files in unintended locations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path comes from a command-line argument and is resolved before directory creation. The behavior is intentional, local, and non-malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Archive Creation from Local Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: package-skill.py recursively packages files from a selected skill directory into a zip file. This can include local files under that directory, so users should review package contents before sharing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The archive operation is explicit and restricted to the provided skill path traversal. No network transfer or hidden destination was found.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent skill packages that are easy for AI agents to load and use. This skill provides templates, workflow guidance, validation, and packaging steps for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code skill creation.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md structure and frontmatter fields.
Guides users through planning skill triggers, workflows, resources, and examples.
Provides templates for skills and subagent configuration files.
Includes a script that initializes a new skill directory with example resources.
Includes validation and packaging scripts for local skill folders.
Advises when to use scripts, references, and assets in a skill package.
limitations: It does not guarantee marketplace approval or security acceptance.
The validation script checks only basic structure and metadata.
Generated templates still require domain expertise and manual editing.
Packaging can include any files under the selected skill directory.

use_cases.title: Create a new reusable skill
use_cases.description: Plan the skill purpose, generate a starter directory, and replace placeholders with useful instructions.

use_cases.title: Standardize team skill authoring
use_cases.description: Use shared templates and quality checks to keep internal skills consistent across projects.

use_cases.title: Package a skill for distribution
use_cases.description: Validate a completed skill folder and create a zip archive that preserves the expected directory structure.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a clear skill idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill creator to help me make a skill for [task]. Ask only the most important setup questions first.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Skill Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these example requests and recommend which instructions, scripts, references, and assets this skill should include: [examples].

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Refine a draft skill for better triggering and usefulness.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing skill draft for trigger clarity, workflow quality, resource structure, and missing examples.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Distribution
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this skill folder, identify risks or placeholder content, and prepare the package for distribution.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for rotating and combining PDF files.
output: A proposed skill name and description.
A workflow outline for PDF operations.
Recommended scripts for deterministic PDF handling.
A checklist for validating and packaging the skill.

output_examples.input: Improve my company policy skill draft.
output: Recommendations to move long policy text into references.
Specific anti-patterns to remove before publishing.
A reminder to inspect included files before sharing.

best_practices: Start from concrete user requests before writing reusable instructions.
Keep SKILL.md focused and move long details into reference files.
Delete unused template files before packaging or sharing a skill.
anti_patterns: Do not leave TODO placeholders in published skill files.
Do not add scripts when simple instructions are enough.
Do not package secrets, private data, or unrelated files with a skill.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create structured AI skills with metadata, instructions, reusable resources, validation, and packaging steps.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it generate a starter skill folder?
faq.answer: Yes. The included initializer script creates SKILL.md and example scripts, references, and assets folders.

faq.question: Does it fully validate skill quality?
faq.answer: No. The quick validator checks basic structure. Human review is still needed for quality, safety, and usefulness.

faq.question: Why does this skill include scripts?
faq.answer: Scripts provide repeatable setup, validation, and packaging behavior that is more reliable than rewriting commands each time.

faq.question: What should users check before packaging?
faq.answer: Users should remove placeholders, verify metadata, inspect bundled files, and ensure no secrets or unrelated files are included.
